name: Team Moore-Miller
subject: The start of the 2025 legislative session
body:
Friend,

Yesterday, the Maryland General Assembly began its 2025 Legislative Session. Over the next ninety days, the Moore-Miller administration will work in partnership with the General Assembly and the people of Maryland to address some of the biggest challenges facing our state.

The 2025 session will be one of the most challenging and consequential. Maryland is facing two coming storms: A new federal administration in Washington that casts uncertainty over our future, and a $3 billion budget gap – our worst fiscal crisis in two decades.

To address these challenges, Governor Moore has built an agenda focused on economic growth. Our plan is designed to do the following:

* Make Maryland an attractive place to live and grow. We will invest in areas like public safety, education, and health care so families and businesses want to come here and stay here.
* Create jobs and grow the middle class. We will continue to strengthen our housing market, reform the procurement process, and ensure that pathways to work, wages, and wealth are available to all Marylanders.
* Invest in sectors of the future. We will implement an all-of-government approach to prioritize commercial hubs and industries that are critical to future growth, including life sciences, I.T., and aerospace and defense.

You cannot have a competitive state without a growing economy. When we took office, Maryland’s economy was stagnant. Our administration is using a fiscally disciplined approach to get Marylanders back to work and jumpstart our economy.

Partnership produces progress. In this legislative session, the Moore-Miller Administration will work closely with legislators, state and local leaders, stakeholders, activists, and advocates to navigate these two storms, grow our economy, and position Maryland to win the decade.

There is a lot of work ahead of us, and we’ll only accomplish these goals with popular support. Together, we can do so much for the people of Maryland. If you agree, will you add your name to our petition to show your support for the Moore-Miller 2025 legislative plan? [https://support.wesmoore.com/a/wes-em-20250109-1?source=em20250109-1]

Add your name » [https://support.wesmoore.com/a/wes-em-20250109-1?source=em20250109-1]

Thank you,
Team Moore-Miller

Baltimore, MD 21211
clean_body: Friend, Yesterday, the Maryland General Assembly began its 2025 Legislative Session. Over the next ninety days, the Moore-Miller administration will work in partnership with the General Assembly and the people of Maryland to address some of the biggest challenges facing our state. The 2025 session will be one of the most challenging and consequential. Maryland is facing two coming storms: A new federal administration in Washington that casts uncertainty over our future, and a $3 billion budget gap – our worst fiscal crisis in two decades. To address these challenges, Governor Moore has built an agenda focused on economic growth. Our plan is designed to do the following: Make Maryland an attractive place to live and grow. We will invest in areas like public safety, education, and health care so families and businesses want to come here and stay here. Create jobs and grow the middle class. We will continue to strengthen our housing market, reform the procurement process, and ensure that pathways to work, wages, and wealth are available to all Marylanders. * Invest in sectors of the future. We will implement an all-of-government approach to prioritize commercial hubs and industries that are critical to future growth, including life sciences, I.T., and aerospace and defense. You cannot have a competitive state without a growing economy. When we took office, Maryland’s economy was stagnant. Our administration is using a fiscally disciplined approach to get Marylanders back to work and jumpstart our economy. Partnership produces progress. In this legislative session, the Moore-Miller Administration will work closely with legislators, state and local leaders, stakeholders, activists, and advocates to navigate these two storms, grow our economy, and position Maryland to win the decade. There is a lot of work ahead of us, and we’ll only accomplish these goals with popular support. Together, we can do so much for the people of Maryland. If you agree, will you add your name to our petition to show your support for the Moore-Miller 2025 legislative plan? [] Add your name » [] Thank you, Team Moore-Miller

IL House GOP Leader McCombie, Transgender Rights Advocate, Earns $145,000 Salary to Push Far-Left Policies, Accept ‘Woke” Donations (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/il-house-gop-leader-mccombie-transgender-rights-advocate-earns-145000-salary-to-push-far-left-policies-accept-woke-donations.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=130308f140-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/il-house-gop-leader-mccombie-transgender-rights-advocate-earns-145000-salary-to-push-far-left-policies-accept-woke-donations.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=130308f140-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
House Speaker Emanuel “Chris” Welch, D-Hillside, shakes hands with House Minority Leader Tony McCombie, R-Savanna, at the House of Representatives’ 2025 inauguration ceremony. (Capitol News Illinois photo by Andrew Adams)
By Illinois Review

Illinois House Republican Minority Leader Tony McCombie earns an annual salary of $145,000 – collecting an additional $52,000 as leader on top of her $93,000 salary as a legislator – making her one of the highest paid politicians in the state.

Illinois lawmakers remain some of the highest paid in the country.

While lawmakers may spend more than 60 percent of their time doing legislative work, the position is considered quasi full-time, meaning many lawmakers have other jobs when they are not in session. Some lawmakers run full-time law firms and real estate firms, while others work in marketing and business development since they are only in session around five months out of the year.
CLICK TO READ FULL ARTICLE ON FACEBOOK AND "LIKE" AND SHARE WITH YOUR FRIENDS (https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1DwuDt4Q3b/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=130308f140-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
During a Wednesday radio interview On The Real with Peggy Hubbard, IR editor-in-chief Mark Vargas had a question for McCombie: since she supports expanding transgender patient rights in Illinois – does she also support men playing in women’s sports?
https://youtu.be/9F5EdT71i8o?si=d5I50Qg7PrmSoAyG&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=130308f140-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
On the Real with Peggy Hubbard
Vargas also discussed the controversy on the hit show, The StoneZONE with Roger Stone, where he shares co-host duties. Stone has served as a senior campaign aide to three Republican presidents – Richard Nixon, Ronald Reagan and Donald Trump and remains a close friend and ally of the 45th and 47th President of the United States. President Trump frequently shares Stone’s posts on his personal Truth Social account.
https://youtu.be/ExTpDRzCRuQ?si=ZW51z2IxlHsm6gqa&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=130308f140-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823

subject: The New York Sun’s Urgent Mission
body:
As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.
It's a voice America needs

---
# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Reader,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.
---

name: Marina Jenkins, NDRC
subject: 2025 is not 2017
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Last year’s House results more closely reflect the will of the people — and that’s exactly what we’ve been fighting for all along.
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100128591/521853463/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjcwNzI1OTEwLWU0Y2UtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=MXupmngk7cqXmm5WSnuJ-oi5U-bAps9OybgMvR8u93U=&emci=52a4c08c-bcce-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=70725910-e4ce-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=8431045)
---

Well, Peter, we’re just about a week and a half out from Inauguration Day. How are you holding up? I’ve been having a lot of conversations with the NDRC community — including our staff, volunteers, and supporters — about the reality of the next four years. It’s a frustrating moment. **But here’s a truth that I hope fills you with a bit of optimism and motivation: We’re more prepared to face threats under the second Trump presidency than the first.** In 2017, despite just _narrowly_ winning the congressional popular vote, the GOP secured a significant House margin — one that, inflated by gerrymandering, meant Donald Trump and House Republicans could pass a more extreme agenda. (They could pass a bill even when twenty House Republicans voted against it!) But _this_ time, thanks in large part to the work of this fair maps community, the House margin more closely reflects the will of the people. This time, just a _handful_ of Republicans could put a stop to any legislation in the House. Take a look at how you and fair maps have helped level the playing field: | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100128592/521853464/1531424108?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_2025Not2017_email_20250109-NDRC-Tier1-2025Not2017_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250109-NDRC-Tier1-2025Not2017_&utm_campaign=2025Not2017&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjcwNzI1OTEwLWU0Y2UtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=MXupmngk7cqXmm5WSnuJ-oi5U-bAps9OybgMvR8u93U=)
---

Fairer maps lead to fairer outcomes — and that’s exactly what we’ve been fighting for all along. And heading into the 2026 midterms, thanks to the work we have done together, Democrats will be in a strong position to compete for the majority.
**Peter: We have a plan that works. And we'll keep building on it to defend democracy from threats under the Trump administration.[If you’re committed to protecting what we’ve fought for and continuing to combat ongoing gerrymandering, please make a contribution to the NDRC right now.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100128593/521853465/1531424108?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_2025Not2017_email_20250109-NDRC-Tier1-2025Not2017_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250109-NDRC-Tier1-2025Not2017_&utm_campaign=2025Not2017&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjcwNzI1OTEwLWU0Y2UtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=MXupmngk7cqXmm5WSnuJ-oi5U-bAps9OybgMvR8u93U=)**
It’s a tough time but if we stay focused and execute our plan, I know we will continue to find wins for democracy.
More soon.
Thanks,
Marina
Marina Jenkins
Executive Director of the NDRC
[DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100128594/521853466/1531424108?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_2025Not2017_email_20250109-NDRC-Tier1-2025Not2017_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250109-NDRC-Tier1-2025Not2017_&utm_campaign=2025Not2017&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjcwNzI1OTEwLWU0Y2UtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=MXupmngk7cqXmm5WSnuJ-oi5U-bAps9OybgMvR8u93U=)

clean_body: Last year’s House results more closely reflect the will of the people — and that’s exactly what we’ve been fighting for all along. | Well, Peter, we’re just about a week and a half out from Inauguration Day. How are you holding up? I’ve been having a lot of conversations with the NDRC community — including our staff, volunteers, and supporters — about the reality of the next four years. It’s a frustrating moment. But here’s a truth that I hope fills you with a bit of optimism and motivation: We’re more prepared to face threats under the second Trump presidency than the first. In 2017, despite just narrowly winning the congressional popular vote, the GOP secured a significant House margin — one that, inflated by gerrymandering, meant Donald Trump and House Republicans could pass a more extreme agenda. (They could pass a bill even when twenty House Republicans voted against it!) But this time, thanks in large part to the work of this fair maps community, the House margin more closely reflects the will of the people. This time, just a handful of Republicans could put a stop to any legislation in the House. Take a look at how you and fair maps have helped level the playing field: | = Fairer maps lead to fairer outcomes — and that’s exactly what we’ve been fighting for all along. And heading into the 2026 midterms, thanks to the work we have done together, Democrats will be in a strong position to compete for the majority. Peter: We have a plan that works. And we'll keep building on it to defend democracy from threats under the Trump administration.= It’s a tough time but if we stay focused and execute our plan, I know we will continue to find wins for democracy. More soon. Thanks, Marina Marina Jenkins Executive Director of the NDRC = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account.
